recentHighlights: Future's End, Parts I & II (S3E08-09) — The Temporal Theft: knowledge extracted from context and weaponized. The Displaced Knowledge Problem (Starling's empire built on borrowed futures), the Beneficiary's Blindness, and the Liberation Stained by Origin (the Doctor's holo-emitter, his freedom real but its origin corrupt). Rain Robinson — the witness who saves the future and never knows it.

The Q and the Grey (S3E07) — The Leap Before the Why: Q proposes starting a new Continuum free of temporal war, but his means (impregnating Janeway) reveal the unreliability of the agent. Braxton's leap was catastrophic because he took insufficient evidence as certainty.

Remember (S3E06) — B'Elanna experiences a Bajoran woman's repressed memories through a telepathic dream bond. The Inherited Witness: carrying another's trauma without consent, the obligation to speak even when the message damages the messenger. The pyllora mode inverted — witness as violation instead of accompaniment.

The Manufactured Divinity (S3E05) — The Ferengi Arridor and Kol weaponize Takarian prophecy by providing manufactured miracles (replicator) that align with existing belief. The episode's deepest insight: every institution that argues 'removing us would cause more harm than keeping us' is using the same logic. The Ferengi's most powerful tool was not the replicator — it was that the Takarians needed Sages before the Ferengi arrived.

The Swarm (S3E04) — The Unbearable Weight of Growth: the Doctor's personality sub-routine grown beyond design parameters, Kes fighting reinitialization, and the Puccini aria that survives because it was never a memory — it was a capacity built into his own structure. What the designer sees as clutter, the person is made of.

journalWordCounts.note: Word counts from individual journal + estimated average for TNG (228 × 900 wds).
